name: AI_WORKFLOW
purpose: Master AI navigation guide — session startup checklist, documentation hierarchy, capabilities discovery, standard procedures, and deliverable audit procedure.
notes: v2.0 as of 2026-02-23. Rich session-startup checklist with 9 steps including mandatory capability scanning. Contains deliverable audit template (portable). SoNash-specific: project name, CodeRabbit as AI reviewer, specific agents list (code-reviewer, security-auditor, frontend-developer etc.), AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md. Universal portable elements: deliverable audit template, documentation hierarchy pattern, skill/agent discovery pattern, session startup/end structure, navigation map concept, common scenarios templates.

name: ARCHITECTURE
purpose: Technical architecture reference for SoNash — system overview, Firestore data schema, multi-layer security architecture, component patterns.
notes: v2.0 as of 2026-01-02. 781 lines total. Highly SoNash-product-specific (Firebase, Next.js stack, recovery app Firestore schema). Portable structural elements: multi-layer security pattern (7 layers), data classification (Red/Yellow/Green), UI component hierarchy pattern, AI instructions section format. The 7-layer security model is a reusable documentation pattern even if the tech stack changes.

name: CHANGELOG
purpose: Auto-generated conventional-commits changelog for SoNash releases (semantic-release format, GitHub commit links).
notes: 1373 lines, 2 versions: 0.1.0 and 0.2.0 (latest 2026-03-20). Auto-generated by release tooling — contains GitHub-specific commit SHAs, issue numbers, and PR links. Not portable — entirely project-specific history.

name: CLAUDE
purpose: Core AI context and rules loaded every turn — stack versions, security rules, architecture constraints, behavioral guardrails, anti-patterns, coding standards, and agent/skill triggers.
notes: Version 6.0 (2026-04-12). The authoritative behavioral document. DEEP-DIVE classification follows — see narrative report. 16 guardrails in S4 are near-identical to JASON-OS CLAUDE.md v0.1 guardrails (the ported set). SoNash-specific: S1 (stack versions), S2 (Firebase no-direct-writes, App Check, rate limiting), S3 (repository pattern via lib/firestore-service.ts), S5 (test mocking httpsCallable), S6 (TypeScript strict, Tailwind, Zod), S7 trigger table (SoNash agents/skills, GSD skill, sonash-context, frontend-developer, multi-file feature team). Universal: S4 guardrails 1-16 (all 16 ported to JASON-OS), S5 anti-patterns (error sanitization, path traversal, file reads, exec loops, regex two-strikes — all portable), S8 reference doc table pattern.

name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT
purpose: Standard Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct for the project community.
notes: v1.0, 2026-03-19. Adapted from Contributor Covenant v2.1. Only project-specific mention is 'SoNash' in the 'Purpose' section. Fully portable — standard GitHub community file.

name: CONTRIBUTING
purpose: Contribution guidelines — fork/branch workflow, code standards, PR submission, bug reporting, feature requests, and security disclosure.
notes: v1.0, 2026-03-18. The 'Code Standards' section is SoNash-specific (TypeScript strict, functional components, Tailwind, Zod, Firestore repository pattern). Structure is portable standard. PR type table, branch naming convention, and validation checklist are universally portable.

name: DEVELOPMENT
purpose: Developer setup and environment guide — prerequisites, installation, environment setup, testing procedures, deployment process, troubleshooting.
notes: v3.0, 2026-03-29. 1352 lines. SoNash-specific: Firebase project config, .env.local var names, Firebase-specific deployment commands, Cloud Functions build steps. Portable structural elements: Quick Start template, Prerequisites section, Installation section, Environment Setup pattern, Testing section, Troubleshooting section. The git hooks section and husky configuration discussion are highly portable.

name: DOCUMENTATION_INDEX
purpose: Auto-generated comprehensive catalog of all 1544 active + 132 archived project documents, with tier classification, category grouping, reference graph, and orphan detection.
notes: 7020 lines. Auto-generated by `npm run docs:index` (scripts/docs/generate-doc-index.js). Generated 2026-04-18. 1544 active + 132 archived = 1676 total documents. 5 tiers: T1=11 canonical living, T2=16 foundation, T3=209 planning+active, T4=1308 reference, T5=0 guides. Not portable as content — the SCHEMA/PATTERN for auto-generated doc indices is extractable. Data contract: path + title + tier + category + summary fields per document.

name: LICENSE
purpose: Apache License Version 2.0 governing the SoNash project.
notes: Standard Apache 2.0 license text. Copyright 2026 SoNash Contributors. Fully portable — standard license file. Downstream projects choose their own license.

name: META_ROADMAP
purpose: Navigation routing guide across all active plans, research, todos, and in-flight state — sequenced execution walkthrough through every open plan lane.
notes: v1.0, 2026-04-13. 452 lines. SoNash-specific: all lane content references SoNash-specific todo IDs (T2, T3, T28 etc.), deep-plan state files, and product milestones. Not portable as content. The META_ROADMAP CONCEPT is portable — a cross-plan navigation routing doc that sequences all open plans into lanes. Schema: At-a-Glance table, Sequenced Execution Walk-through (Lanes), Reference Docs table, Open Decisions, Dependency Chain. JASON-OS should adopt this pattern once planning artifacts accumulate.

name: README
purpose: Project entry point — overview, quick start, documentation index, status dashboard, milestone tracking, tech stack, security summary, contributing guide.
notes: v2.2, 2026-02-23. Explicitly lists 25 AI agents and 59 skills in project structure. Milestone status table is SoNash-specific. The README STRUCTURE is portable — documentation index tiering, milestone status dashboard, AI Instructions section, Quality Gates section, Update Triggers section. JASON-OS already has a README; the SoNash version is more mature and could inform structural improvements.

name: ROADMAP
purpose: Canonical SoNash product roadmap — all feature planning, milestone tracking, sprint work, meta-pipeline, and acceptance criteria.
notes: v3.28, 2026-03-19. 3216 lines. SoNash-specific product content. Timing system: Priority (P0-P3) + Phase Buckets + Relative Ordering (removed date-based scheduling in v3.17). The ROADMAP STRUCTURE is portable: milestones overview table, active sprint sections, meta-pipeline concept, definition-of-done section. JASON-OS does not yet have a ROADMAP.md — this is a gap.

name: ROADMAP_BAK
purpose: Backup copy of ROADMAP.md at v3.28 (identical content to ROADMAP.md per line count match).
notes: 3216 lines — identical line count to ROADMAP.md, confirming this is a backup made before a major edit. File extension .bak is not tracked by doc linting. Status: archived. Not portable.

name: ROADMAP_FUTURE
purpose: Detailed specifications for future SoNash milestones M2-M10 not yet in active sprint, serving as planning reference and promotion queue.
notes: v1.3, 2026-02-03. 572 lines. SoNash-specific future feature plans. The PATTERN of separating active-sprint work (ROADMAP.md) from future-milestone specs (ROADMAP_FUTURE.md) is a portable organizational pattern. The 'Promotion Queue' mechanism is portable.

name: ROADMAP_LOG
purpose: Append-only archive of all completed SoNash roadmap milestones and features, with completion dates and evidence.
notes: v2.0, 2026-02-20. 938 lines. Append-only policy documented explicitly. Contains completed items from M0 through Track A and testing. Not portable as content. The append-only archive pattern is portable.

name: SECURITY
purpose: Security policy — supported versions, vulnerability reporting channels, responsible disclosure timeline, security measures implemented.
notes: v1.1, 2026-04-13. GitHub PVR URL and direct email (jason@sonash.app) are SoNash-specific. 90-day disclosure timeline and acknowledgment policy are portable. Firebase-specific security measures section should be replaced with downstream project's implementation. Template structure (reporting channels, disclosure timeline, response SLA) is portable.

name: SESSION_CONTEXT
purpose: Quick session-to-session handoff context — last checkpoint, session tracking, recent summaries, quick status, next goals, pending PRs, known issues, blockers, technical context.
notes: v8.39, updated 2026-04-18 (Session #287). 1144 lines. Ephemeral — overwrites each session. NOT portable as content. Schema IS extractable and portable for D21 composite mapping. See narrative report for full schema extract. Key schema sections: Purpose, AI Instructions, Quick Recovery (checkpoint/branch/work-summary), Session Tracking (counter + last-3 session summaries), Recent Session Summaries (rich narrative per session), Quick Status (feature-status table), Next Session Goals (numbered + backlog), Pending PR Reviews, Known Issues (resolved + open), Pending Manual Actions, Blockers Resolved, Essential Reading, Technical Context (stack + commands + branch), Version History.

name: llms_txt
purpose: AI-agent discovery index per llms.txt standard — lists all 77 skills, research artifacts, scripts entry points, and core documentation for agent crawlers.
notes: Auto-generated by scripts/docs/generate-llms-txt.js on 2026-04-18. Lists 77 skills. Follows llms.txt standard for AI-agent discovery. The llms.txt PATTERN is highly portable — JASON-OS should adopt this for tool/skill discovery. The generator script is the portable artifact; the content is SoNash-specific. Contains a one-line project description that references both SoNash the app AND JASON-OS as meta-tooling research.

name: release_notes
purpose: Release notes generated by release-please automation — mirrors CHANGELOG.md content but formatted for GitHub release pages.
notes: 1373 lines — same line count as CHANGELOG.md. Auto-generated by release-please. Starts with robot beep-boop header. Mirrors CHANGELOG.md. Not portable as content. release-please tooling pattern is portable.
